body:
Title: CRA Form T2036 2025 Provincial or Territorial Foreign Tax Credit.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2036/t2036-25e.pdf

CRA T2036 2025 calculates the provincial or territorial foreign tax credit for non-Quebec residents. Line 1 enters the amount from line 1 of Form T2209. Line 2 enters the amount from line 3 of Form T2209 unless the taxpayer must pay minimum tax and a special calculation applies. Line 3 is line 1 minus line 2. Line 4 multiplies net foreign non-business income divided by net income by provincial or territorial tax otherwise payable. Line 5 is the lesser of line 3 or line 4, should not be more than provincial or territorial tax otherwise payable, and is entered as the provincial or territorial foreign tax credit on Form 428 or T2203 as applicable.

T2036 note 1 describes minimum-tax line 2 special calculations. British Columbia residents enter line 3 of Form T2209. Ontario residents use allocation formulas based on line 1, total foreign taxes paid, and the special foreign tax credit from line 11 of Part 4 of Form T691, with a different formula depending on whether total non-business income taxes paid to all foreign countries are $200 or less or more than $200. Residents of another province or territory enter the part of the special foreign tax credit that relates to non-business income taxes paid to a foreign country. T2036 note 2 says net foreign non-business income is the amount from line 2 of Form T2209. Note 3 says net income is the amount from line 2 of Form T2209, with part-year resident and multiple-jurisdiction adjustments. Note 4 describes how to determine provincial or territorial tax otherwise payable from Form 428 or T2203.
